sanskrit:
यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता॥
transliteration:
yadā saṃharate cāyaṃ kūrmo 'ṅgānīva sarvaśaḥ |
indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā ||
literal_meaning: When this [person] withdraws the senses from sense-objects, just as a tortoise retracts its limbs entirely, his awareness is firmly established.
contextual_meaning: True psychological maturity is the capacity to voluntarily disengage attention from external stimuli; the mind becomes steady when sensory input is no longer compulsively pursued.
psychological_state: Mindful inhibition of bottom-up sensory capture; prefrontal governance over limbic reactivity.
trigger_scenario: You instinctively reach for your phone during a lull; noticing the impulse, you leave it untouched and feel an immediate surge of mental space.
anchor_text: Pull your senses inward like a tortoise; steady awareness rises when cravings fall silent.
